title: Korean Bulgogi-Style Chicken

ingredients.name: boneless chicken (thigh or breast), thinly sliced

subs.name: 300 g mushrooms and 200 g tofu, sliced thin and marinated the same way — reduce cook time to 5 minutes
tamari for a slightly less salty flavour; use the same quantity
2 tbsp ripe pineapple juice or 1 tbsp grated raw papaya — both tenderise equally well

steps.text: Grate the apple half and squeeze out most of the juice. Combine with soy sauce, sugar, minced garlic, grated ginger, black pepper and gingelly oil to form the bulgogi marinade. The apple tenderises the chicken and adds mild sweetness.

Slice the chicken thinly across the grain into pieces about 3–4 mm thick — thin slices cook in seconds and absorb the marinade deeply. Add to the marinade, toss to coat every piece and rest for 10 minutes at room temperature.
Slice the spring onion whites on a slight diagonal; reserve the greens for garnish.

Heat oil in a tawa or wide kadai on high flame until the pan is smoking hot. Lay the marinated chicken in a single layer — resist moving it for 90 seconds to develop a light char on the underside. Toss and cook 4–5 minutes more until the edges are caramelised and no pink remains inside.

Add the spring onion whites in the last minute of cooking and toss once. Remove from heat, scatter sesame seeds and spring onion greens over the chicken. Serve immediately alongside steamed rice and a simple cucumber salad.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Verify poultry doneness with a food thermometer where possible; do not rely on colour alone.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: chicken → 300 g mushrooms and 200 g tofu, sliced thin and marinated the same way — reduce cook time to 5 minutes; apple → 2 tbsp ripe pineapple juice or 1 tbsp grated raw papaya — both tenderise equally well; soy-sauce → tamari for a slightly less salty flavour; use the same quantity
indian_kitchen_adaptation: A tawa is ideal for bulgogi chicken — the wide, flat surface encourages the sugary marinade to caramelise into a glaze rather than steam. Keep the flame high and cook in batches if the tawa is small; crowding the pan causes steaming rather than charring. Apples are widely available in Indian markets; the grated fruit is a traditional Korean tenderiser. Gingelly oil (nallennai/til ka tel) and soy sauce are stocked at Indian provision stores and supermarkets.
